headline: KLIA2 Terminal Exceeds 45 Million Passenger Capacity
context: KLIA Terminal 2 handled 48 million passengers in 2025 against design capacity of 45 million causing chronic congestion.

cards.big: KLIA2 exceeded 45 million capacity handling 48 million passengers in 2025
cards.sub: Immigration queues averaged 75 minutes during peak hours throughout 2025

cards.big: AirAsia processed 38 million of 48 million KLIA2 passengers in 2025
cards.sub: Airline demanded RM2.8 billion expansion or relocation to dedicated facility

cards.big: MAHB proposed RM4.5 billion KLIA2 expansion adding 30 million more capacity
cards.sub: Construction timeline of 5 years means relief would not arrive until 2031

cards.big: Flight delays at KLIA2 increased 35% with average delay of 42 minutes
cards.sub: Ground handling bottlenecks and gate shortages cited as primary constraints

cards.big: Capacity crisis tests whether airport planning can keep pace with aviation growth
45 million passengers at KLIA2 — a terminal designed as budget now operates as a primary hub.
